original_hypothesis.title: GaugeExtraRewarder Uses TOKEN.balanceOf(GAUGE) Instead of _totalSupply Creating Reward Distribution Mismatch
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: GaugeExtraRewarder.updatePool and _pendingReward use IERC20(IGauge(GAUGE).TOKEN()).balanceOf(GAUGE) as lpSupply for reward calculations. However, GaugeV2 uses its internal _totalSupply for main reward calculations. If someone sends LP tokens directly to the gauge without calling deposit(), TOKEN.balanceOf(GAUGE) > _totalSupply. This dilutes extra rewards for legitimate stakers while main rewards remain unaffected, creating an asymmetry between reward streams that can be exploited.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: An attacker can donate LP tokens to the gauge to inflate balanceOf(GAUGE), diluting extra rewards per share without affecting their main gauge rewards. The cost is the donated LP tokens; the gain is reduced extra reward distribution to other stakers.

proof_of_signal.pattern: uint256 lpSupply = IERC20(IGauge(GAUGE).TOKEN()).balanceOf(GAUGE); -- uses actual balance instead of gauge._totalSupply

preconditions: GaugeExtraRewarder is set as gaugeRewarder for a gauge
Attacker has LP tokens to donate
Extra rewards are actively being distributed

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: balanceOf(GAUGE) used instead of gauge._totalSupply for reward calculation denominator
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Donating tokens to gauge inflates denominator, diluting rewards for all stakers
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: Should use gauge.totalSupply() instead of TOKEN.balanceOf(GAUGE)

analysis_questions: How large is the typical extra reward compared to main rewards?
What is the cost/benefit of LP token donation attack?
Can the donated tokens be recovered via skim or sync?
validation_requirements: Compare balanceOf(GAUGE) vs gauge._totalSupply after donation
Calculate reward dilution percentage per donated LP
Test reward claim after donation to verify reduced amounts

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Attacker sends 1000 LP tokens directly to gauge (without deposit). balanceOf(GAUGE) increases by 1000 but _totalSupply stays the same. Extra rewards are now divided by a larger denominator, reducing rewards per share for all stakers. Attacker's main gauge rewards are unaffected since they use _totalSupply.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Reduced extra rewards for all legitimate stakers proportional to donation size
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All gauge stakers receiving extra rewards
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Donated LP tokens stuck in gauge; effect persists until tokens removed

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test comparing balanceOf vs _totalSupply impact on extra rewards

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Detectable by comparing TOKEN.balanceOf(GAUGE) vs gauge.totalSupply()

validation_result.mechanism: lpSupply denominator mismatch between GaugeExtraRewarder and GaugeV2
validation_result.impact_summary: GaugeExtraRewarder uses TOKEN.balanceOf(GAUGE) as the reward denominator while GaugeV2 uses internal _totalSupply. Direct LP token donations to the gauge inflate the extra reward denominator, diluting extra rewards for all stakers without affecting main rewards.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: GaugeExtraRewarder.updatePool() and _pendingReward() both compute lpSupply as IERC20(IGauge(GAUGE).TOKEN()).balanceOf(GAUGE). This reads the actual ERC20 balance of the LP token held by the gauge contract. However, GaugeV2.rewardPerToken() uses _totalSupply (internal accounting variable) for its reward-per-token calculation. Under normal operation these values are equal, but anyone can send LP tokens directly to the gauge via ERC20.transfer() without calling deposit(). This increases balanceOf(GAUGE) but NOT _totalSupply, creating a divergence. The extra rewarder then uses a larger denominator than it should, reducing accRewardPerShare growth rate and diluting extra rewards for all legitimate stakers. Meanwhile, main gauge rewards (using _totalSupply) are unaffected. GaugeV2 exposes totalSupply() as a public view function, so the GaugeExtraRewarder could have used that instead.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: The proof_of_signal is verified. GaugeExtraRewarder.sol uses IERC20(IGauge(GAUGE).TOKEN()).balanceOf(GAUGE) in both updatePool() and _pendingReward(). GaugeV2.sol uses _totalSupply in rewardPerToken(). There is no mechanism in GaugeV2 to skim excess LP tokens or reconcile balanceOf with _totalSupply. The defect is a clear inconsistency in how the reward denominator is derived across the two contracts.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: lpSupply is derived from balanceOf(GAUGE) which includes any directly donated tokens. Should use IGauge(GAUGE).totalSupply() to match GaugeV2's internal accounting.
Same issue as updatePool - uses balanceOf(GAUGE) instead of totalSupply(), enabling donation-based dilution of extra rewards.
GaugeV2 correctly uses _totalSupply (internal accounting) for main reward distribution, creating an asymmetry with GaugeExtraRewarder which uses balanceOf.

execution_path_analysis.entry_point: Any ERC20 transfer of LP token to gauge address
calling_sequence: 1. Attacker calls TOKEN.transfer(GAUGE, donationAmount) - LP tokens sent directly to gauge
2. TOKEN.balanceOf(GAUGE) increases by donationAmount, but GaugeV2._totalSupply unchanged
3. On next GaugeExtraRewarder.updatePool() call, lpSupply = balanceOf(GAUGE) > _totalSupply
4. accRewardPerShare increment is smaller: reward * ACC_TOKEN_PRECISION / (inflated lpSupply)
5. All stakers receive proportionally less extra rewards
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: GaugeExtraRewarder.updatePool() reads lpSupply via IERC20(IGauge(GAUGE).TOKEN()).balanceOf(GAUGE). This value includes any LP tokens directly transferred to the gauge without calling deposit(). The denominator in the accRewardPerShare calculation is thus inflated, reducing the per-share reward accumulation. GaugeV2's rewardPerToken() uses _totalSupply which only changes via deposit/withdraw/depositsForGenesis, creating an asymmetric reward dilution between main and extra rewards.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts provided for GaugeExtraRewarder specifically. The defect is inherent in the contract code regardless of deployment configuration.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: Attack is straightforward: (1) Attacker obtains LP tokens (e.g., by providing liquidity). (2) Attacker transfers LP tokens directly to the gauge contract address via TOKEN.transfer(GAUGE, amount). (3) TOKEN.balanceOf(GAUGE) increases but GaugeV2._totalSupply does not. (4) Future calls to GaugeExtraRewarder.updatePool() use the inflated lpSupply, reducing accRewardPerShare growth. (5) All legitimate stakers receive diluted extra rewards. The donated LP tokens are permanently stuck in the gauge since there is no skim function. The cost to the attacker is the donated LP tokens; the effect is proportional dilution of extra rewards for all stakers.
validation_result.impact_assessment: Medium severity. Extra rewards for all gauge stakers are diluted proportionally to the donated LP amount. If an attacker donates X LP tokens and the gauge has Y staked via deposit(), the dilution factor is Y/(X+Y). Main gauge rewards are unaffected. The donated tokens are permanently lost, creating a cost barrier. However, the attack is persistent - once done, the dilution remains indefinitely unless tokens are somehow recovered. This could be strategically used to grief competing stakers or reduce their yield.
recommended_mitigations: Replace IERC20(IGauge(GAUGE).TOKEN()).balanceOf(GAUGE) with IGauge(GAUGE).totalSupply() in both updatePool() and _pendingReward() to match the gauge's internal accounting
Alternatively, add a skim function to GaugeV2 that allows recovering excess TOKEN balance beyond _totalSupply
Consider adding a view function to GaugeV2 that exposes _totalSupply specifically for the extra rewarder
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy Pair, PairFactory, GaugeV2, GaugeExtraRewarder in test environment. 2. Set GaugeExtraRewarder as gaugeRewarder on GaugeV2. 3. Fund extra rewarder and call setDistributionRate. 4. User A deposits 1000 LP via gauge.deposit(1000). 5. Advance time, record extra rewards earned by A. 6. Attacker transfers 1000 LP directly to gauge via TOKEN.transfer(gauge, 1000). 7. Advance same time period, record extra rewards earned by A. 8. Assert rewards in step 7 are approximately half of step 5 (diluted by 50%). 9. Verify main gauge rewards are unchanged between steps 5 and 7.
uncertainties: The practical impact depends on the value of extra rewards relative to main rewards
The cost-benefit ratio for an attacker depends on LP token value vs extra reward value
It's unclear if there are any off-chain monitoring systems that would detect balance/supply divergence
additional_testing_needed: Quantify the economic impact by modeling realistic extra reward amounts and LP token values
Test whether the donation can be recovered by any privileged function not visible in scope
Verify the interaction between genesis pool deposits and the balanceOf discrepancy
